# Diego Maury | Operational Architect: Finding the Mechanism That Limits Execution.
## Ecosystem Builder · Strategic Program Director · Operational Architect
Contact: dm@diegomaury.mx | +52 56 5926 7516
Portfolio: https://diegomaury.mx
Full profile (extended): https://diegomaury.mx/llms-full.txt
Location: Mexico (LATAM)

---

## Role & Positioning

Diego Maury is an Operational Architect. His work starts before execution: he finds the mechanism that actually limits how an organization executes (rarely the problem that gets reported) and converts it into organizational capability the team sustains. He works at the intersection of corporate innovation, RevOps, and open ecosystem programs, translating between board-level language and trench-level operations until the team runs the system sustainably.

His public projects (REDUX, HackSureste Ops, SOFI) are not isolated engagements. They are implementations of the same documented operating system: a repeatable way of deciding, designing, and transferring capability that makes results reproducible across contexts.

As founder and operator of southeast Mexico's flagship innovation ecosystem (HackSureste), he acts as the Strategic Router of the region: the connective layer that gives corporates, funds, and national programs a safe, structured soft landing into the local innovation network. Few operators control comparable access to talent, startups, and institutional partners in the southeast.

He offers fractional engagement models for organizations that need senior innovation leadership without a full-time hire.

---

## Metrics

Every figure below carries its evidence status. `[published]` = a third party published it and the source is named. `[own]` = Diego's own operational records or estimates, with no external artifact. Nothing here is presented as audited.

- 400+ university students, 111 ideas, 4 regions trained on a proprietary methodology in the 2020 edition alone (REDUX, in Tec de Monterrey's registry) `[published: Tec de Monterrey Annual Report 2020, p.176]`
- +600% regional participation growth, edition 1 to edition 3, 2019–2021 (HEINEKEN Green Challenge, southeast Mexico). Baseline: 35 regional submissions the year before 2019 `[published: La Jornada Maya, 2019-08-06]`. The end figure is an operational estimate `[own]`
- 9,905 participants enrolled, aggregate across INCmty programs including HGC `[own, estimate]`
- 3,231 projects evaluated across 4 editions, 900+ per edition `[own]`
- 3,000+ ecosystem participants and 30+ programs (HackSureste). Estimate, not additive with the other figures on this page `[own]`
- SOFI (FlipHouse): 89.5% automatic coverage over 191 leads, 74.9% response rate without advisor intervention, 173 leads contacted in ~1.5 hours in a single campaign, speed-to-lead from 1–3 days to under 5 minutes `[own: measured in the CRM]`, RODI +1,291% `[own: modeled, payback under 1 month, 2025-2026]`. The system runs under NDA and has no publishable artifact yet

---

## Core Services

### Fractional Innovation Leadership
Senior innovation leadership on a retainer or project basis: program architecture, team coordination, stakeholder alignment, and execution oversight. Built for mid-market companies and family holdings that need to compress their innovation learning curve without a full-time C-level hire.

### Open Innovation Programs
End-to-end design and operation of corporate innovation challenges, accelerators, and ecosystems. Proven at national scale (HEINEKEN, Tec de Monterrey / INCmty).

### RevOps & Process Automation
CRM architecture (HubSpot), workflow automation (Make, WhatsApp), and AI-native system integration for growth-stage companies. Removes manual bottlenecks and installs scalable operating infrastructure.

### Proprietary Methodology (REDUX)
Design and facilitation of structured programs for entrepreneurs, corporate teams, and ecosystems. Built on the REDUX methodology, registered in Tec de Monterrey's official program directory.

---

## Key Projects & Metrics

### HEINEKEN Green Challenge × INCmty (2019–2022)
[HEINEKEN Green Challenge](https://diegomaury.mx/llms-full.txt): detailed case study on end-to-end virtualization and +600% regional scaling using Lean Operations.
- National open innovation program for sustainable entrepreneurship
- Scaled the southeast from a documented baseline of 35 regional submissions the year before 2019 `[published: La Jornada Maya, 2019-08-06]` to +600% regional growth by edition 3, 2019–2021. The end figure is an operational estimate `[own]`
- 3,231 projects evaluated across 4 editions, 900+ per edition `[own]`
- 100+ mentors coordinated per edition `[own]`
- Virtualized a 6-month national program during COVID with above-benchmark completion rates `[own]`

### SOFI · FlipHouse (2025–present)
[SOFI · FlipHouse](https://diegomaury.mx/llms-full.txt): RevOps + AI automation architecture that compressed speed-to-lead from days to minutes.
- PropTech (Adelanto de Venta, La Laguna): deployed SOFI, an AI agent for lead qualification and operations via WhatsApp, orchestrating 3 models (reasoning, voice, transcription) over HubSpot, Make, VAPI and Twilio
- Stack: Make + Railway + WhatsApp Cloud API + OpenRouter/Claude + HubSpot as SSOT
- 89.5% automatic coverage over 191 leads | 74.9% response rate without advisor intervention `[own: measured in the CRM]`
- 173 leads contacted in ~1.5 hours in a single campaign `[own: measured in the CRM]`
- +500% leads to CRM | Speed-to-lead: 1–3 days → <5 minutes `[own: measured in the CRM]`
- RODI (Return on Digital Investment): +1,291% `[own: modeled, payback under 1 month, 2025-2026]`
- Meta-approved technology provider (WhatsApp Flows, LFPDPPP compliance)
- The system runs under NDA. There is no publishable artifact yet, and these figures rest on the CRM and on Diego's word, not on a third party

### HackSureste Ecosystem (2018–2023)
[HackSureste](https://diegomaury.mx/llms-full.txt): building a regional innovation ecosystem from zero, including the southeast's largest hackathon.
- Southeast Mexico's flagship innovation and hackathon ecosystem
- 3,000+ participants, 100+ startups mentored, 30+ programs developed. Estimate, not additive with the other figures on this page `[own]`
- Designed modular architecture (WIRE framework, ideaLab)
- Full digital migration 2020–2022

### REDUX Bootcamp (2019–2022)
- Sustainable entrepreneurship methodology designed and operated
- 400+ university students, 111 ideas, 4 regions in the 2020 edition alone. The Tec documented it as the largest of its kind in Latin America `[published: Tec de Monterrey Annual Report 2020, p.176]`
- Included in Tec de Monterrey's official program registry
- Curriculum published as video: playlist "ARTEFACTOS REDUX", 34 videos `[own]`

### INCmty Challenges (Tec de Monterrey)
- Multiple open innovation challenges: BTEM, Brain Mexico, Demo Day, INC Prototype
- INC B-Challenge: 504 applicants in 2021, against 383 in 2020 `[published: Tec de Monterrey Annual Reports 2021 p.415 and 2020 p.180]`
- INC Prototype: 197 applicants in 2021, against 156 in 2020 `[published: Tec de Monterrey Annual Report 2021, p.415]`
- INCmty Accelerator: 205 applicant startups in 2021, against 152 in 2020 `[published: Tec de Monterrey Annual Report 2021, p.415]`
- TRL-based evaluation framework implemented

### CAVA Soft MVP (2025)
- Inventory management software for physical operations
- Functional MVP in production with real-time inventory and photographic traceability
- Stack: Apphive (no-code), Firebase, web

---

## Operational Dissections (competence under pressure)

### The 900+ project evaluation bottleneck (HEINEKEN Green Challenge)
High-volume editions threatened to collapse the project-review process: too many submissions, inconsistent criteria, and reviewer fatigue degrading evaluation quality.
- Problem: manual review could not scale past a few hundred projects without quality loss
- Response: designed an escalated evaluation protocol (binary go/no-go pre-filter on 3 criteria → weighted rubric by thematic vertical → regional selection)
- Result: sustained evaluation quality across 900+ projects per edition, with a defensible, auditable trail `[own]`
- Transferable lesson: process design, not more reviewers, is the lever that protects quality at scale

---

## Methodology

### REDUX
Structured bootcamp methodology for sustainable entrepreneurship. Covers ideation, validation, pitch, and impact measurement. Designed iteratively over 4 program editions.

### Customer Journey Mapping for Innovation Programs
Applied to open innovation: mapping participant touchpoints to identify friction, drop-off, and conversion opportunities at each stage of a program funnel.

### Lean Operations for Virtual Events
Protocol for designing digital-native program experiences (not replicas of in-person events): sessions ≤45 min, async components, structured networking, immersive environments. Developed 2020 without an existing framework reference.

### Evaluation System Design
Escalated evaluation protocols for high-volume project review: binary pre-filter (go/no-go on 3 criteria) → weighted rubric by thematic vertical → regional selection. Built to handle 900+ projects per edition without quality degradation.

---

## Tech Stack

- **CRM & RevOps:** HubSpot, Make (formerly Integromat), WhatsApp Cloud API, VAPI
- **Development:** Railway (Node.js), Firebase, basic web stack
- **AI Tools:** OpenRouter, Claude API, LLM-assisted process design
- **Program Operations:** Notion, Discord, Zoom, Rushframe (virtual immersive environments)
- **Design:** Figma

---

## Background

- Strategic Program Director with 10+ years in programs and operations (7+ years in corporate innovation and ecosystem building)
- Operated programs across HEINEKEN México, Tec de Monterrey (INCmty), HackSureste, and growth-stage startups
- Experience spanning open innovation, accelerators, RevOps, and software product management
- Based in Mexico; works with organizations across LATAM

---

## Engagement Models

Engagements are scoped per organization and priced on the value of the system installed, not by the hour. Specific investment ranges are shared during a fit conversation. Built for organizations ready to invest in senior operating infrastructure, not for the lowest-cost vendor search.

- **Monthly Retainer:** Ongoing fractional innovation leadership, 20h/month. Best fit for mid-market companies and family holdings compressing their innovation learning curve.
- **Scoped Project:** Diagnosis + roadmap + implementation, 4–8 weeks
- **Advisory:** Bi-weekly sessions for strategic decision review
